name: Pepperoni Sticks - Italian Style
recipeInstructions:
Ingredients for 100 pounds.
Meat in Pounds Boneless cowmeat: 50 pounds, Beef cheeks: 20 pounds, Beef flanks or plates: 30 pounds.
1. Grinding and Mixing:
- Grind all the meats through a 3/16-inch grinder plate.
- Remove to mixer and add all the ingredients, mixing evenly.
- Regrind through an 1/8-inch grinder plate. The meat is now ready for stuffing.
2. Stuffing:
- It is essential that the meat is well chilled to avoid smearing the meat.
- Stuff the meat into 24 to 26 mm lamb casings.
3. Smoking:
- Preheat smokehouse to 125 degrees F and place pepperoni in smoker.
- Have dampers wide open without smoke until the casings are dry.
- Close the dampers 1/4 open and apply a heavy smoke, raising the temperature to 140 degrees.
- Hold this temperature until the internal temperature of the product reaches 130 degrees.
- When using PORK, raise the temperature to 150 degrees and hold until the internal temperature reaches 138 degrees.
- Remove from the smokehouse and chill with cold tap water until the internal temperature is reduced to 105-110 degrees.
- Return to the cold smokehouse at 110 degrees and apply a heavy smoke for at least 12 hours or overnight.
4. Drying:
- The drying room should be kept at 60-65 degrees and a relative humidity of 70%.
- The drying time will vary because of the amount of moisture that has been removed from the sausage during smoking.
- It usually takes 3 to 4 days to make a satisfactory pepperoni.
- When fully dry, the pepperoni will yield from 50 to 55% of the original product.
Alternate Drying Method without Smoking:
- Hold pepperoni at 70 degrees for about 2 days, maintaining a relative humidity of about 75%.
- The product should be kept in a cooler for at least 20 days from the time the cure was added to the pepperoni.
- Be sure that casings used are not over 1-3/8 inches in diameter as this formula applies only to casings below this range.
